steps.title: Identify the passage's structure
steps.content: The passage describes one approach: Indigenous politicians in the US joined established parties and won congressional seats. Then "on the other hand" introduces a second approach: Indigenous people in Canada and Latin America formed their own parties. The passage contrasts two distinct strategies for achieving political representation — that contrast is the whole point.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — To trace the history of an Indigenous political movement and speculate about its future development: The passage covers only 2000–2021 and discusses two separate movements, not one; it makes no predictions. ✗
  • Choice B — To argue that Indigenous politicians in the United States should form their own political party: The passage makes no such argument; both strategies are presented neutrally and both produced results. ✗
  • Choice C — To highlight two approaches to achieving political representation for Indigenous people: The passage explicitly contrasts working within established parties (US) versus forming independent parties (Canada, Latin America). ✓
  • Choice D — To consider how Indigenous politicians in the United States have influenced Indigenous politicians in Canada and Latin America: The passage never suggests any influence between the US and other countries — the contrast stresses difference, not influence. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
